title: rubus × iddabergensis
intro: Rubus × iddabergensis, also known as the Idaho blackberry, is a hybrid plant species resulting from the cross between Rubus idaeus (European red raspberry) and Rubus allegheniensis (Allegheny blackberry). This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it beneficial for soothing wounds and reducing inflammation. The health benefits of Rubus × iddabergensis include its ability to support digestive health, reduce fever, and act as a natural antiseptic. Rubus × iddabergensis can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and salves, which are often used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and infections.
